title: elaeodendron lanceolatum
intro: Elaeodendron lanceolatum, also known as the lance-leaf elephant ear tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. The plant's bark and leaves have been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in the Pacific Islands, for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Elaeodendron lanceolatum include its potential to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic. Elaeodendron lanceolatum is often prepared for medicinal use through infusion of the leaves and bark in hot water, as well as being used in traditional remedies and herbal preparations.
